seoTitle: Orient Point vs Osborne — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Orient Point and Osborne. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Orient Point vs Osborne: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Orient Point and Osborne.
heading: Orient Point vs Osborne — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Orient Point, New South Wales and Osborne, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Orient Point has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Osborne (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Orient Point or Osborne safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Orient Point has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Osborne scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Orient Point scores 31/100 for bushfire risk. Osborne scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Orient Point or Osborne?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Orient Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.28% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
